{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "critical",
  "work_stage": "in-progress",
  "component_conditions": ["non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 40,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Stored hydraulic energy not isolated, blocked, or bled before commencing service",
    "Gravity-stored energy not released or mechanically blocked",
    "Capacitor stored electrical energy not discharged or verified zero-energy",
    "LOTO procedure incomplete — energy source missed during isolation verification",
    "Zero-energy state not confirmed for all energy types per OSHA 1910.147"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop all work immediately and evacuate the danger zone",
    "Apply full LOTO to all energy sources including stored/residual energy",
    "Bleed, vent, or release stored hydraulic pressure and verify zero pressure",
    "Install mechanical blocks or supports to neutralize gravity-stored energy",
    "Discharge capacitors and verify zero voltage with a rated meter",
    "Verify zero-energy state for every energy source before resuming work",
    "Revise the LOTO procedure to include all stored energy types and retrain personnel"
  ],
  "rationale": "An energy source was missed during LOTO — specifically stored hydraulic, gravity, and/or capacitor energy. OSHA 1910.147 requires all energy sources including stored/residual energy to be isolated, dissipated, or blocked before work begins. Work is actively in progress with uncontrolled stored energy present, creating imminent risk of serious injury or death from unexpected energization, sudden release of hydraulic pressure, gravity-driven movement, or capacitor discharge. This is a critical non-compliance requiring immediate work stoppage.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA 1910.147(c)(1) - energy isolation procedure must cover all energy sources",
    "OSHA 1910.147(d)(5) - release of stored or residual energy",
    "OSHA 1910.147(d)(6) - verification of isolation before work begins"
  ]
}
